what_it_does: Implements two sibling screens for a Counterpart studio app: TemplatesScreen, which lets users browse, filter, search, inspect, and seed projects from templates, and FilesScreen, which lists studio image assets with kind filters.
observations: Uses global React hooks renamed useStateT, useMemoT, and useEffectT.
Exposes TemplatesScreen and FilesScreen on window.
Reads templates, categories, and files from window.MS_DATA.
Reports workspace context via window.ViContext.set in both screens.
TemplatesScreen receives category and search state through props so persistence is handled externally.
Featured templates sort first and display a star badge; the grid remains stable across category changes.
TemplateCard is a button that opens a detail view; the actual seed action is inside TemplateDetail.
TemplateDetail renders a modal scrim with Escape-to-close handling, a 'What's inside' list, meta data, and Use/Close actions.
FilesScreen is a flat table with thumbnails, kind badges, model, author, and date.
Helper functions catLabel and metaLine format category names and template metadata.
standouts: The deliberate separation between opening a template to inspect it and pressing 'Use this template' to seed work.
A single stable grid with featured-first sorting instead of a disappearing featured band.
Two distinct screens, Templates and Files, co-located in one module.
conventions: CSS BEM-like class prefixes: tpl, tplc, tdetail, files.
Functional components with aliased React hooks.
Global window registration for screen components.
Event stopPropagation on modal and Escape handler to contain interaction.
concerns: No loading or empty-data guards beyond a zero-match message.
FilesScreen builds a CSS class from f.kind.split(' ')[0].toLowerCase(), which is fragile if kind labels change.
TemplateCard and TemplateDetail read window.MS_DATA during render without memoization or error handling.
TemplateDetail's Escape handler is registered with an empty dependency array, so onClose is captured once and could become stale if the prop changes.
No type checking or runtime schema validation for template or file objects.
notes: A browse-and-inspect templates page paired with a minimal flat file browser, both wired to global data and context.
questions: Where are onUseTemplate, onCat, onQuery, and onKind defined and how is routing handled?
Is Icon a global component or imported from another module?
What consumes window.ViContext and what schema does it expect?
Are template fields such as anchors, prompts, states, bundles, and rules always numeric counts?
Why are TemplatesScreen and FilesScreen maintained in the same file?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a dedicated global Templates page and a flat Files view; the file matches that purpose, though it also mixes two screens in one module.
intention_signals: The file is trying to provide a calm, predictable starting-point marketplace where users inspect templates before committing, while also offering a minimal inventory view of studio assets.
novelty: The deliberate separation of card-open (inspect) from 'Use this template' (seed), plus a stable single-grid featured-first sort that avoids layout jumps.
contribution: A reusable templates browsing and inspection UI pattern and a flat files list, including modal detail composition, filter/search wiring, and global context reporting hooks.
summary_for_embedding: React screen module exposing window.TemplatesScreen and window.FilesScreen that browse template cards with category/search filters and a detail modal, and list studio files by kind, reading from window.MS_DATA and updating window.ViContext.
intention_for_embedding: To give users a global starting-point gallery where they inspect templates before seeding new work, alongside a simple asset file browser.

symbols.does: Renders a template card button that opens a detail view.
symbols.description: Displays cover art or a fallback icon, category badge, use count, title, description, a two-item meta line, and a View prompt; clicking the card triggers onOpen without launching any work.

symbols.does: Renders a modal detail view for a selected template with a use action.
symbols.description: Shows enlarged art, category, title, description, a 'What's inside' list built from present numeric fields, author/update/use meta, Escape-to-close handling, and buttons to use the template or close the modal.

symbols.does: Closes the template detail modal when Escape is pressed.
symbols.description: Keyboard event handler attached inside TemplateDetail that stops propagation and invokes onClose when the Escape key is pressed.

symbols.does: Looks up a human-readable category label from global data.
symbols.description: Finds the matching entry in data.templateCategories by id and returns its label, falling back to the raw id if not found.

symbols.does: Builds a short metadata string for a template card.
symbols.description: Collects counts of states, bundles, rules, anchors, and prompts from a template, then joins the first two available items with a dot separator.
